title: Selmer groups and a Cassels-Tate pairing for finite Galois modules
speaker: Alexander Smith
abstract: I will discuss some new results on the structure of Selmer groups of finite Galois modules over global fields. Tate's definition of the Cassels-Tate pairing can be extended to a pairing on such Selmer groups with little adjustment, and many of the fundamental properties of the Cassels-Tate pairing can be reproved with new methods in this setting. I will also give a general definition of the theta/Mumford group and relate it to the structure of the Cassels-Tate pairing, generalizing work of Poonen and Stoll.

proof_stages.label: Classical Selmer and Cassels-Tate motivation
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls group cohomology, global and local fields, Shafarevich-Tate groups, abelian-variety n-Selmer exact sequences, and the classical Cassels-Tate pairing, then reframes it as measuring liftability between Selmer groups.

proof_stages.label: Selmer category with local conditions and duality
proof_stages.summary: A general Selmer group is attached to a Galois module with local conditions, organized into a category SMod_F so that Selmer is functorial and Cartier duality with local Tate orthogonality gives the correct dual object.

proof_stages.label: General Cassels-Tate pairing for exact sequences
proof_stages.summary: After defining exact sequences in SMod_F to remove trivial local lifting obstructions, the generalized Cassels-Tate pairing is stated with its left kernel, and the dual exact sequence gives the corresponding right-kernel and duality identity.

proof_stages.label: Naturality and Baer-sum linearity
proof_stages.summary: The pairing is shown to be natural under morphisms of exact sequences, and because SMod_F admits Baer sums, the Cassels-Tate pairing becomes additive in the extension class as well as bilinear in the two Selmer inputs.

proof_stages.label: Symmetry and antisymmetry from self-duality
proof_stages.summary: Combining naturality with the duality identity yields symmetry when the comparison map is self-dual and antisymmetry when it is anti-self-dual, recovering the classical principally polarized abelian-variety case.

proof_stages.label: Theta-group control of diagonal terms
proof_stages.summary: The talk distinguishes antisymmetric from alternating Q/Z-valued pairings and uses Poonen-Stoll theta groups, generalized to central extensions, to compute the diagonal of a Cassels-Tate pairing via a theta class.

proof_stages.label: Class groups and reciprocity as Selmer/Cassels-Tate pairings
proof_stages.summary: A reciprocity theorem of Lipnowski-Sawin-Zimmerman is recast by viewing the dual class group and the class group as Selmer-type objects and identifying the nth-order reciprocity pairing with a Cassels-Tate pairing.

proof_stages.label: Class-group symmetry and CM theta refinements
proof_stages.summary: Self-dual root-of-unity identifications, Baer sums, and field-change ideas prove the class-group symmetry results, while theta-group diagonal calculations give finer CM-field sign statements involving complex conjugation.
